Overview Install Quickstart API Reference CLI SDK Authentication Webhooks

Globex SDK Getting Started

This guide walks you through installing the Globex SDK and making your first authenticated request in under five minutes.

Prerequisites

You need a Globex account and an API key generated from the dashboard. Node.js 18+ and npm 9+ are required for the JavaScript SDK.

Install

Install the SDK from the public npm registry. Production teams should pin a major version. The package supports CommonJS and ESM imports.

Authenticate

Authentication is handled via the GLOBEX_API_KEY environment variable. The SDK refuses to start if the key is missing or malformed.

Make a request

Every SDK method returns a typed Promise. Errors are thrown as GlobexAPIError instances carrying the HTTP status code and the raw response body.

Handle webhooks

Webhook payloads are signed with HMAC-SHA256. Verify the signature before trusting the payload — the SDK ships a helper for this.

Next steps

Read the API reference for every supported endpoint, or jump into the CLI guide if you prefer a no-code workflow.
